title: Mortgage Pre-Approval in Greater Vancouver
metaDescription: Get a fully underwritten mortgage pre-approval with a 90–120 day rate hold from a Greater Vancouver mortgage broker. Free, no obligation, no hard credit pull required.
intro: A real pre-approval is the difference between hopefully shopping and confidently making offers. We'll lock in a rate, run your file through a real lender, and give you a number you can take to a realtor.

body.heading: Pre-qualification vs. pre-approval — they aren't the same
paragraphs: A pre-qualification is a soft estimate based on what you tell me about your income, debts and down payment. No documents, no credit pull, no commitment from a lender. It's useful as a 15-minute starting point.

A pre-approval is a fully underwritten file. We collect your paystubs, T4s, NOAs, ID, and down-payment proof, run a credit pull, and submit to a specific lender for an actual rate hold — typically 90 to 120 days. When you find a home, we already have most of the underwriting done.

body.heading: What a real pre-approval gets you
paragraphs: Realtors take pre-approved buyers seriously. Sellers in multiple-offer scenarios across Greater Vancouver routinely pick the offer with a clean pre-approval over a stronger price with no financing certainty. The pre-approval letter is the document your realtor includes with the offer.
bullets: A specific maximum mortgage and purchase price
A rate hold that protects you against increases for 90–120 days
A clear list of any conditions still outstanding
Confidence to make subject-free offers when warranted

whoItsFor: Plan to shop for a home in the next 0–4 months
Want a real number, not a guess
Want a rate hold to protect against rate increases
requirements: Two recent paystubs (or 2 years of T4s / NOAs / financials if self-employed)
90-day account history showing down payment funds
List of any current debts, loans or alimony/child support

process.detail: We talk through your goals and confirm pre-approval is the right step.
Secure upload of everything we need — usually takes you 30 minutes.
I submit to the right lender for your file and lock in the rate hold.
Within 24–72 hours you have a pre-approval letter for your realtor.

faqs.question: Does a pre-approval hurt my credit?
faqs.answer: There's a single hard credit pull when we underwrite. Multiple mortgage credit pulls within a 30-day window are typically grouped as one inquiry by the credit bureaus, so the score impact is minimal — usually 3–8 points, recovering within a few months.

faqs.question: How long is a rate hold good for?
faqs.answer: Most rate holds are 90 to 120 days. If you don't buy within the hold period, we re-submit and lock a new hold — there's no penalty for letting one expire.

faqs.question: Can I take a lower rate if it appears during my hold period?
faqs.answer: Often yes — most lenders will float you down to a lower rate within their family of products if rates drop before your closing date. That's a question I always negotiate up front.

faqs.question: What if my situation changes during the hold?
faqs.answer: Tell me immediately. A change in employment, debts or down payment can affect approval, and it's far easier to address proactively than at the closing finish line.
